decision_summary: The chip peak temperature (89.26°C) exceeds the limit (85.0°C). The most effective primary lever is to increase the heat spreader conductivity to enhance lateral heat spreading away from the chip hot spot. I will apply a conservative 1.5x multiplier to the current spreader conductivity, moving it from 396.9 to 595.35 W/(m*K), which remains within the allowed bounds and step ratio.

changes.reason: Increasing spreader conductivity improves lateral heat diffusion, reducing the local thermal gradient under the chip and lowering the peak temperature.

expected_effects: Reduction in chip_max_temperature towards or below the 85.0°C constraint.
More uniform temperature distribution across the heat spreader layer.
risk_notes: Diminishing returns may occur if the bottleneck shifts to the base plate conductivity or boundary conditions rather than lateral spreading.
Ensure the new conductivity value does not create numerical stiffness issues, though the LU solver should handle this range.
